seo.title: AI Agent Memory Management in Claude Tool Use Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent memory management in Claude Tool Use agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Claude Tool Use agents, AI Agent Memory Management problems manifest as agents losing track of prior tool results, context history, or decision states during multi-turn interactions, leading to repetitive tool calls and inconsistent reasoning. This is exacerbated by Claude's stateless message API where memory must be manually maintained across `messages.create()` calls, causing drift in long-running agent loops. Common issues include forgotten tool outputs, hallucinated prior states, and context overflow from unpruned conversation histories.

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-step research agent calling web_search tool repeatedly
howItManifests.symptom: Agent calls web_search with identical queries despite having results in prior messages
howItManifests.impact: Infinite loops, API quota exhaustion, and incomplete task execution

howItManifests.scenario: Code generation agent with iterative debugging
howItManifests.symptom: Forgets previous error messages and fixes, regenerating same broken code
howItManifests.impact: Stuck in debugging loops, producing inconsistent code versions

howItManifests.scenario: Database query agent building complex JOIN queries
howItManifests.symptom: Loses track of which tables were already queried, re-querying same data
howItManifests.impact: Performance degradation, duplicate data processing, incorrect aggregations

howItManifests.scenario: Customer support agent handling multi-turn conversations
howItManifests.symptom: References incorrect prior customer details or conversation history
howItManifests.impact: Customer frustration, incorrect resolutions, trust erosion

detection.name: Duplicate Tool Call Detection
detection.description: Scans conversation history for identical tool calls with matching parameters within session

detection.name: Memory Reference Drift Check
detection.description: Detects when agent references non-existent prior tool results or conversation states

detection.name: Context Window Overflow Monitor
detection.description: Tracks token count and detects when conversation history exceeds safe memory limits

fixes.name: Tool Result Caching Layer
fixes.description: Cache tool results with TTL and inject summaries into context instead of full history

fixes.name: Conversation Pruning Strategy
fixes.description: Automatically prune old messages while preserving critical tool results and decisions

fixes.name: State Persistence Middleware
fixes.description: Extract and persist structured agent state across API calls

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Memory Management Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade observability for Claude Tool Use agents by automatically wrapping `client.messages.create()` calls with `vex.verify()`. This captures full conversation traces, tool call sequences, and memory state transitions, enabling detection of duplicate tool calls, memory drift, and context overflow in real-time. Vex's agent-specific assertions validate that tool results are referenced correctly and conversation history remains coherent across turns.

The platform automatically flags memory management issues with precise callstack traces and provides one-click remediation templates. Vex maintains a baseline of healthy agent behavior and alerts when memory patterns deviate, preventing cascading failures before they impact production workflows. Integration takes under 5 minutes with the `vex-sdk` and works seamlessly with Anthropic's native tool use APIs.
vexAutomation.code:
```python
from vex import verify
from anthropic import Anthropic

client = Anthropic()
tool_cache = ToolMemoryCache()

@verify(
    assert_no_duplicate_tool_calls=True,
    assert_memory_coherence=True,
    max_context_tokens=150000,
    agent_name="research_agent"
)
def reliable_claude_agent(messages, tools):
    # Inject cached tool summaries
    summaries = [tool_cache.get_summary(tid) for tid in recent_tool_ids]
    if summaries:
        messages[0]['content'].append({
            'type': 'text',
            'text': 'RECENT TOOL RESULTS:\n' + '\n'.join(summaries)
        })
    
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=messages,
        tools=tools,
        tool_choice="auto"
    )
    
    # Cache new tool results
    if response.stop_reason == 'tool_use':
        for tool_use in getattr(response, 'tool_use_contents', []):
            tool_cache.store_result(
                tool_use.id,
                tool_use.input,
                tool_use.name
            )
    
    return response
```
